references.text: [ErSt71b] Erd\H{o}s, P. and Straus, E. G., Solution to Problem. Amer. Math. Monthly (1971), 302-303.

remarks.text: Erd\H{o}s and Straus \cite{ErSt71b} have proved the existence of some constant $c>0$ such that\[-c < k(N)-(e-1)N \ll \frac{N}{\log N}.\]

statement.text: Let $N\geq 1$ and let $k(N)$ denote the smallest $k$ such that there exist $N\leq n_1<\cdots <n_k$ with\[1=\frac{1}{n_1}+\cdots+\frac{1}{n_k}.\]Is it true that\[\lim_{N\to \infty} k(N)-(e-1)N=\infty?\]
